Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 11:02 ET
Bullish stock-picking meets index hedging as CPI looms in negative gamma regime
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. Extreme flow divergence shows institutions buying single-name calls while hedging with index puts — classic dispersion play ahead of CPI.
  • 2. Negative $6.7B SPY gamma amplifies all moves with VIX term structure in contango signaling complacency despite dealer short positioning.
  • 3. COIN massive call blocks and PLUG explosion suggest crypto/clean energy rotation as institutional 13F reversals play out in options flow.
NARRATIVE — dispersion before CPI

The most striking signal today is the extreme divergence between index puts (1.54 P/C) and single-name calls (0.89 P/C) — a 62-point spread indicating institutions are buying stock-specific upside while hedging systematic risk ahead of Wednesday's CPI print. This dispersion setup makes sense given implied correlation at 0.19, the lowest stock-specific regime reading, where single-name volatility trades more efficiently than index vol. The 150,000 SPY May 500 put block at $0.84 provides systematic downside protection 24% out-of-the-money, while aggressive call buying in COIN (24,180 contracts across April and June 170 strikes) and the PLUG explosion (+97% volume, 0.10 P/C) suggest selective bullish positioning in crypto and clean energy themes. Negative $6.7B SPY gamma creates a dangerous amplification environment where any directional move gets magnified by dealer hedging, yet VIX term structure remains in +0.6 contango suggesting complacency about near-term risks. This setup becomes particularly concerning with CPI in three days — historically the most volatile single data point for equity markets. The vol surface shows 4 names in backwardation (MRVL, TSLA, XLE, XLF) pricing elevated near-term fear, while multiple divergence flags indicate structural tensions between cheap vol/rich skew conditions across IBIT, XLY, NFLX, and MU.

Layer 1: Sentiment +12Layer 2: GEX -$6.7BLayer 3: P/C split 62ptLayer 4: IC 0.19Layer 9: CPI 3d
